adds.rationale: Opens quantum-wave exposure via a diversified ~86-holding equal-weighted ETF rather than a single pre-revenue pure-play, after Q1 2026 quantum earnings (IonQ +755% YoY revenue, Rigetti ~200% YoY, D-Wave record $33M bookings) confirmed commercial traction across the sub-sector.

news_evidence.summary: IonQ Q1 2026 revenue grew 755% YoY with raised full-year guidance; Rigetti Q1 revenue up ~200% with 108-qubit Cepheus-1 at general availability; D-Wave record $33.4M bookings up nearly 2,000% YoY including $20M Florida Atlantic University sale.
news_evidence.source: Zacks / TipRanks Q1 2026 quantum earnings roundup

news_evidence.summary: Defiance Quantum ETF (QTUM) tracks the BlueStar Quantum Computing and Machine Learning Index across ~86 holdings using modified equal-weight (caps ~2% per name), expense ratio 0.40%, AUM over $3.6B in early 2026.

adds.rationale: Adds direct exposure to the AI-custom-silicon and AI-networking leg of the hyperscaler buildout that NVDA-plus-TSM does not fully capture, after Broadcom's Q1 FY26 print showed AI revenue +106% YoY to $8.4B, a $73B AI backlog, and OpenAI added as a sixth named custom-silicon customer.

news_evidence.summary: Broadcom Q1 FY26 (reported March 4, 2026): total revenue $19.31B (+29% YoY); AI semiconductor revenue $8.4B (+106% YoY); Q2 AI revenue guided to $13.1B; CEO Hock Tan cited 'clear line of sight' to >$100B annual AI chip revenue by late 2027/early 2028.
Broadcom expanded named custom-silicon customer list from 5 to 6 with OpenAI joining Google, Anthropic, Meta, and two unnamed hyperscalers; AI backlog cited at $73B.
news_evidence.source: FinancialContent coverage of Q1 FY26 print

removes.rationale: Nuclear bucket is at the 4-ticker diversity ceiling; NUKZ (broad nuclear-equity ETF) overlaps materially with CCJ/URA on uranium and adds the least incremental thesis exposure relative to LEU (HALEU enrichment, SMR-fuel critical) and the two uranium names, so trimming it keeps the nuclear thesis intact at 3 focused names and frees a slot for a named next-wave bet.

news_evidence.summary: Q1 2026 SMR-thesis news (Meta-Oklo 1.2 GW Ohio campus announced Jan 2026; NuScale-TVA 6 GW program) is captured by single-name nuclear holdings; a broad-basket nuclear ETF does not add differentiated exposure beyond CCJ + LEU + URA.

removes.rationale: AI bucket would exceed the 4-ticker cap with AVGO added; TSM is the weakest direct AI exposure of the four mega-caps (foundry rather than AI compute/software end-demand owner) and carries the most geopolitical-tail risk, so swapping TSM for AVGO sharpens AI exposure toward custom silicon plus hyperscaler-software without expanding ticker count.

news_evidence.summary: Bank of America's 2026 AI-chip buy list names NVDA, AVGO, MRVL, AMD, AMAT, LRCX, CDNS, SNPS as the eight primary beneficiaries of the upgraded $1.3T 2026 semiconductor TAM; TSM appears in foundry rolls but is one step removed from AI-compute end-demand owners.
news_evidence.source: Bank of America 2026 semiconductor outlook / Yahoo Finance summary

rationale_overall: Net effect: watchlist stays at the 12-ticker cap but rotates from broad-basket / indirect exposure toward more thesis-aligned positions. Nuclear concentration drops from 4 to 3 (CCJ, LEU, URA) by removing the broad NUKZ ETF, while quantum exposure opens at 1 (QTUM), filling a named next-wave gap the watchlist had zero exposure to. The AI bucket stays at 4 names (MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, AVGO) but rotates from foundry (TSM) to custom-silicon and AI-networking (AVGO), aligning with the Q1 FY26 hyperscaler-capex evidence. Robotics (ARKQ), rockets (RKLB), and ballast (SPY, AGG) unchanged.
